brief_nursing_elaboration: Severe hyperthyroidism/thyroid storm. Nursing: beta-blockers, PTU/methimazole admin, iodine solution, fever management, cardiac monitoring, anxiolytic support, ICU monitoring.
Thyroid hormone deficiency from iodine/autoimmune. Nursing: levothyroxine admin (empty stomach), TSH monitoring, energy assessment, weight management, cold intolerance education.
Thyroid hormone excess from Graves/toxic nodule. Nursing: beta-blockers, antithyroid meds, iodine solution, symptom management, weight monitoring, exophthalmos care if Graves.
Pancreatic tumor producing excess gastrin (Zollinger-Ellison). Nursing: high-dose PPI admin, H2-blocker support, diarrhea management, imaging coordination, surgical intervention.
Cartilage degeneration in weight-bearing joints. Nursing: pain management, ROM exercises, weight reduction, assistive devices, joint protection, acetaminophen/NSAIDs, ice/heat therapy.
Gallbladder removal for cholelithiasis/cholecystitis. Nursing: post-op pain, bile duct patency, diarrhea management, dietary fat restriction initially, laparoscopic vs open recovery.
Radiographic visualization of bile ducts. Nursing: contrast reaction monitoring, iodine allergy assessment, post-procedure abdominal pain evaluation, pancreatitis precautions.
Pancreatic tumor producing excess ADH (SIADH). Nursing: fluid restriction, sodium monitoring, hyponatremia correction, seizure precautions, gradual sodium correction.
Lymphoid malignancy (type of NHL). Nursing: chemotherapy coordination, CBC monitoring, infection precautions, nutritional support, psychosocial support, hair loss management.
Thyroid removal for cancer/hyperthyroidism. Nursing: post-op hypothyroidism monitoring, hormone replacement, calcium/parathyroid assessment, voice changes, laryngeal nerve damage precautions.
Pyloric muscle hypertrophy obstructing gastric outlet. Nursing: pre-op hydration/electrolyte repletion, post-pyloromyotomy feeding progression, vomiting precautions, metabolic alkalosis management.
Mitral valve area reduction from rheumatic/congenital. Nursing: activity limitation, anticoagulation if afib, diuretic therapy, sodium restriction, surgical intervention assessment.
Aortic valve area reduction from rheumatic/calcification. Nursing: activity restriction, antibiotic prophylaxis, syncope/chest pain assessment, surgical/transcatheter replacement readiness.
Tricuspid valve regurgitation from RV dilation/endocarditis. Nursing: right-sided heart failure assessment, peripheral edema evaluation, diuretic therapy, activity tolerance.

root_2_meaning: Latin: re = back + urgere = to flow
brief_nursing_elaboration: Mitral valve incomplete closure from prolapse/endocarditis. Nursing: afterload reduction, diuretics, anticoagulation if afib, activity tolerance, surgical replacement consideration.
Aortic valve insufficiency from endocarditis/hypertension. Nursing: afterload reduction, vasodilators, activity tolerance, antibiotic prophylaxis, surgical replacement readiness.
Pulmonary valve area reduction (congenital). Nursing: activity assessment, cyanosis monitoring, right heart strain evaluation, balloon valvuloplasty/surgical valve replacement readiness.
TOF (VSD/pulmonary stenosis/RVH/ASD). Nursing: hypercyanotic spell management, activity tolerance, pain assessment, surgical repair timing, post-op cardiac monitoring.
Abnormal opening between ventricles. Nursing: left-to-right shunt assessment, pulmonary/systemic blood flow monitoring, activity tolerance, surgical closure consideration.
Abnormal opening between atria. Nursing: left-to-right shunt assessment, arrhythmia monitoring, activity tolerance, endocarditis prophylaxis, transcatheter/surgical closure readiness.
Failure of ductus arteriosus to close. Nursing: left-to-right shunt assessment, pulmonary edema monitoring, indomethacin/ibuprofen admin, surgical ligation consideration.
Aortic narrowing (usually distal to left subclavian). Nursing: BP differential (upper vs lower extremities), femoral pulse assessment, collateral circulation evaluation, surgical repair assessment.
Aorta arises from RV, pulmonary from LV. Nursing: prostaglandin E1 admin (keep PDA open), balloon atrial septostomy, cyanosis management, surgical arterial switch repair.
LV/aorta/mitral valve hypoplasia. Nursing: prostaglandin E1 admin, oxygen balance optimization, three-stage surgical repair (Norwood/Glenn/Fontan), cardiac output assessment.
No tricuspid valve; ASD/VSD required for survival. Nursing: prostaglandin E1 admin, cyanosis management, three-stage surgical repair (Blalock-Taussig/Glenn/Fontan), oxygen sat monitoring.
No pulmonary valve; PDA required for survival. Nursing: prostaglandin E1 admin, cyanosis management, shunt creation (Blalock-Taussig), balloon valvulotomy/surgical intervention.

prefix_meaning: Named after Wilhelm Ebstein - cardiac pathologist
brief_nursing_elaboration: Tricuspid valve abnormality with displacement. Nursing: arrhythmia monitoring (WPW), right heart failure assessment, activity tolerance, surgical valve repair/replacement consideration.
MI/unstable angina from coronary obstruction. Nursing: continuous cardiac monitoring, troponin/CK-MB serial, ECG assessment, antiplatelet/anticoagulant admin, PCI/fibrinolytic readiness.
Myocardial necrosis from coronary obstruction. Nursing: continuous monitoring, cardiac markers, thrombolytic/PCI coordination, pain management, post-MI rehabilitation.

literal_translation: Unpredictable chest pain from decreased coronary flow
brief_nursing_elaboration: Unstable chest pain from coronary insufficiency. Nursing: continuous monitoring, antiplatelet/anticoagulant admin, troponin monitoring, PCI readiness, risk stratification.

literal_translation: Heart unable to pump adequately with blood backing up
brief_nursing_elaboration: Heart pump failure from systolic/diastolic dysfunction. Nursing: diuretic therapy, ACE inhibitor/beta-blocker admin, fluid restriction, daily weights, activity tolerance assessment.
Interstitial/alveolar fluid from cardiac/non-cardiac. Nursing: high-flow O2, elevate HOB, diuretics, morphine, CPAP/PEEP, pulmonary artery catheter consideration.

literal_translation: Severe sudden lung failure from injury
brief_nursing_elaboration: ARDS from sepsis/aspiration/pneumonia. Nursing: mechanical ventilation, lung-protective ventilation, prone positioning, fluid management, paralytic sedation, stress ulcer prophylaxis.
COPD (emphysema/chronic bronchitis). Nursing: bronchodilator therapy, steroids, O2 therapy, pulmonary rehabilitation, smoking cessation, exacerbation management.
Elevated pulmonary artery pressure. Nursing: pulmonary vasodilators (sildenafil/bosentan), anticoagulation if idiopathic, right heart function assessment, transplant consideration.

literal_translation: Sudden inflammation of kidney filtering units
brief_nursing_elaboration: Post-infectious glomerular inflammation. Nursing: fluid/sodium restriction, diuretics, BP management, urinalysis trending, renal function monitoring, corticosteroid consideration.

literal_translation: Long-term inflammation of kidney filtering units
brief_nursing_elaboration: Progressive glomerular scarring. Nursing: ACE inhibitor/ARB therapy, BP control, fluid restriction, nutritional management, ESRD preparation, dialysis education.
RPGN (anti-GBM/ANCA-associated). Nursing: aggressive immunosuppression, plasmapheresis, corticosteroids, renal function monitoring, biopsy coordination, dialysis readiness.

literal_translation: Membrane and cell proliferation in kidney filtering units
brief_nursing_elaboration: MPGN (most common chronic GN). Nursing: immunosuppressive therapy, corticosteroid/cyclophosphamide admin, renal function monitoring, proteinuria assessment, ESRD preparation.

literal_translation: IgA antibody deposit disease in kidneys
brief_nursing_elaboration: IgA-dominant GN (most common primary GN). Nursing: ACE inhibitor/ARB therapy, BP control, NSAIDs if mild, fish oil, proteinuria reduction, renal biopsy discussion.
GN after streptococcal/viral infection. Nursing: supportive care, fluid management, diuretics, ACE inhibitors, corticosteroids if severe, renal function monitoring, streptococcal treatment.

prefix_meaning: Greek: diabetes = condition of excessive urination
brief_nursing_elaboration: Glomerular injury from hyperglycemia. Nursing: tight glucose control, ACE inhibitor/ARB therapy, BP management, proteinuria monitoring, dietary protein restriction, ESRD preparation.
Immune complex GN from SLE. Nursing: immunosuppressive therapy (corticosteroids/cyclophosphamide/mycophenolate), renal function monitoring, urinalysis assessment, ANA/complement trending.
MN (subepithelial immune deposits). Nursing: corticosteroid/cyclophosphamide therapy, ACE inhibitors, anticoagulation if nephrotic, nephrotic syndrome management, remission monitoring.

literal_translation: Kidney disease with excessive protein loss
brief_nursing_elaboration: Massive proteinuria (>3.5g/day), hypoalbuminemia, edema. Nursing: diuretics, ACE inhibitors, anticoagulation, salt restriction, albumin infusion if severe, immunosuppressive therapy.
